title: carex ampullacea var. utriculata
intro: Carex ampullacea var. utriculata, also known as the bladder sedge or water sedge, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae and is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of Carex ampullacea var. utriculata include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide relief from skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, or salves, which can be applied topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
